headlines.headline: Trump Says He Holds Regular Talks With Putin and Zelenskiy on Ukraine War
headlines.summary: US President Donald Trump revealed he maintains direct communication with both Russian and Ukrainian leaders as he pushes for a settlement to the ongoing conflict. The disclosure comes amid intensified diplomatic efforts.

perspectives.angle: Trump Confirms Separate Calls with Putin and Zelensky on Ukraine
perspectives.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump said he has had "good conversations" with both Russian President Vladimir Putin and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky as de-escalation efforts continue.

headlines.headline: Russian Defence Minister Visits North Korea in Second High-Level Trip
headlines.summary: Russian Defence Minister Andrei Belousov arrived in North Korea for talks, marking the second senior Russian official to visit Pyongyang in recent days. The visit underscores deepening military ties between Moscow and Pyongyang amid the Ukraine war.

perspectives.angle: Russian Duma Speaker Visits Pyongyang for North Korea War Memorial Opening
perspectives.summary: Vyacheslav Volodin arrived in North Korea to attend the opening of a memorial honoring North Korean soldiers killed fighting for Russia in Ukraine. The visit underscores deepening military ties between Moscow and Pyongyang following North Korea's troop deployment.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu's Main Political Rivals Unite to Challenge His Coalition
headlines.summary: Two of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's most formidable political rivals announced they are joining forces ahead of elections expected later this year, in a significant challenge to his long-standing coalition government.

perspectives.angle: Netanyahu's Top Rivals Form Alliance to Challenge His Coalition
perspectives.summary: Two of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's most formidable political rivals announced they are joining forces to contest the upcoming election and oust his coalition government.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Marks 40th Anniversary of Chornobyl Disaster Under War Clouds
headlines.summary: Ukraine commemorated four decades since the world's worst nuclear accident at Chornobyl, with officials warning that Russia's ongoing invasion could trigger another nuclear catastrophe.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine Commemorates 40th Anniversary of Chornobyl Disaster Under Shadow of War
perspectives.summary: Ukraine marked the 40th anniversary of the Chornobyl nuclear disaster on Sunday amid ongoing fears that Russia's war could trigger another nuclear catastrophe. The Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant remains a concern.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine marks Chornobyl 40th anniversary under shadow of war
perspectives.summary: Ukraine commemorated the 40th anniversary of the Chornobyl nuclear disaster amid fears that Russia four-year-old war could trigger another nuclear catastrophe.

headlines.headline: Israel Orders Evacuations of Seven Lebanese Towns After Soldier Killed
headlines.summary: Israel issued evacuation warnings for seven towns in southern Lebanon beyond the Litani River buffer zone after a soldier was killed near the border. The move escalates tensions despite a fragile ceasefire with Hezbollah.

headlines.headline: China Yuan Settlements Hit Record High Amid Iran Conflict
headlines.summary: China Cross-border Interbank Payment System (CIPS) reached a record single-day transaction value of 1.22 trillion yuan, with analysts saying the Middle East conflict has acted as a catalyst for rising demand in yuan settlement, particularly in oil trade.

headlines.headline: Germany Shifts Military Strategy Away From US Dependence
headlines.summary: Germany new military doctrine emphasizes "less America, more Europe" as Berlin prepares for reduced US contributions to European security and takes greater responsibility for its own defense capabilities.

perspectives.angle: Germany Unveils First-Ever Military Strategy, Names Russia Top Threat
perspectives.summary: The Bundeswehr strategy marks Germany's first comprehensive military doctrine since WWII, explicitly identifying Russia as the primary security threat amid shifting European defense posture.

headlines.headline: Two Israeli Soldiers Killed in Jordan Border Crossing Attack
headlines.summary: Two Israeli soldiers were shot dead by a Jordanian aid truck driver near the Allenby (King Hussein) Crossing between the West Bank and Jordan. The attacker was neutralized at the site.

headlines.headline: Iran foreign minister returns to Pakistan as US talks scrapped
headlines.summary: Iran's top diplomat arrived back in Pakistan after planned talks with the US delegation were canceled. Trump says both sides can continue negotiations by phone as diplomatic efforts to end the Iran conflict continue.

perspectives.angle: Iran's Foreign Minister Returns to Pakistan for Key US-Iran Talks
perspectives.summary: Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi arrived back in Islamabad for high-level meetings as Pakistan seeks to revive peace negotiations between the US and Iran after Trump canceled the dispatch of American envoys.

perspectives.angle: Pakistan races to revive stalled US-Iran talks after Trump scraps envoys visit
perspectives.summary: Pakistani officials scrambling to restart negotiations after Trump canceled his envoys trip to Islamabad. Iranian FM Araghchi met Oman sultan to discuss Strait of Hormuz mechanism. Ceasefire holding but economic fallout growing as Brent crude up 50% since war began.

perspectives.angle: Trump cancels US envoy trip to Pakistan for Iran talks
perspectives.summary: President Trump abruptly cancelled plans to send envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to Pakistan for ceasefire talks with Iran, saying "too much time" had been "wasted on traveling" and that Iran had "tremendous infighting."

perspectives.angle: Trump cancels envoys' Pakistan trip for Iran talks, saying US "holds all the cards"
perspectives.summary: President Trump abruptly cancelled Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner's trip to Pakistan for Iran negotiations, telling Fox News "you're not flying 18 hours to sit and talk about nothing." Iranian FM Araghchi left Islamabad without meeting US officials.

perspectives.angle: Iran Foreign Minister Leaves Islamabad Without Meeting US Envoys
perspectives.summary: Irans foreign minister departed Islamabad before US envoys could arrive for talks, highlighting the collapse of Pakistans mediation efforts between the US and Iran.

perspectives.angle: Confusion over Iran talks as Trump cancels envoy trip to Pakistan
perspectives.summary: Iran says no direct talks planned with US despite Trump sending envoys Kushner and Witkoff to Pakistan for negotiations. Tehran foreign ministry denies any US discussions even as Araghchi arrives in Islamabad.

perspectives.angle: US Envoys Head to Pakistan for Iran Talks as Diplomatic Push Intensifies
perspectives.summary: Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will travel to Islamabad on Saturday for negotiations with Iranian officials, as both sides seek to de-escalate the ongoing conflict. Iran's foreign minister Abbas Araghchi is also in Pakistan for the discussions.

perspectives.angle: Iran Rules Out Direct US Talks as Envoys Head to Pakistan
perspectives.summary: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi arrived in Islamabad but Iranian officials say there will be no direct negotiations with Washington, despite US envoys Witkoff and Kushner also traveling to Pakistan for potential indirect talks.

perspectives.angle: Trump envoys Witkoff and Kushner to fly to Pakistan for Iran talks
perspectives.summary: US special envoys will travel to Pakistan for diplomatic discussions as Iran says its Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi is already in Pakistan but no US-Iran meeting is planned.

perspectives.angle: Trump to send Witkoff and Kushner to Pakistan for Iran talks
perspectives.summary: US envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will fly to Pakistan for discussions on Iran, while Irans foreign minister is in Islamabad. Iranian state media reports direct US talks are not on the agenda.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu orders army to attack Hezbollah in Lebanon
headlines.summary: The Israeli PM directed the military to "vigorously attack" Hezbollah in Lebanon after six people were killed by strikes despite a ceasefire being extended by three weeks.

perspectives.angle: Netanyahu accuses Hezbollah of sabotaging Lebanon peace talks
perspectives.summary: Israeli PM says the Iran-backed group is actively undermining diplomatic efforts to reach a ceasefire agreement.

perspectives.angle: Netanyahu warns Hezbollah is dismantling Lebanon ceasefire
perspectives.summary: Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu accused Hezbollah of undermining the ceasefire agreement with Lebanon, asserting Israel will continue operations against threats. IDF struck Hezbollah operatives and infrastructure in south Lebanon.

perspectives.angle: Netanyahu accuses Hezbollah of dismantling Lebanon ceasefire
perspectives.summary: Israeli PM says Hezbollah actions are threatening the ceasefire with Lebanon as sirens warn of suspected drone attacks in northern Israel.

perspectives.angle: Netanyahu Orders Vigorous Attack on Hezbollah After Ceasefire Extension
perspectives.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu directed the military to "vigorously attack" Hezbollah targets in Lebanon, just two days after a ceasefire was extended. At least six people were killed in Israeli strikes on Saturday, with cross-border exchanges straining the truce agreement.

perspectives.angle: Netanyahu Orders IDF Strikes on Hezbollah After Ceasefire Violations
perspectives.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu ordered the IDF to "vigorously" attack Hezbollah in southern Lebanon after the group launched projectiles into northern Israel. The ceasefire extension announced by Trump appears increasingly fragile as both sides exchange fire.

perspectives.angle: Netanyahu Orders Vigorous Attacks on Hezbollah After Ceasefire Strains
perspectives.summary: Israeli PM Netanyahu ordered the military to vigorously attack Hezbollah targets in Lebanon after at least six people were killed in Israeli strikes. Cross-border exchanges have strained the extended ceasefire agreement.

headlines.headline: Mexico says US agents killed in crash were not authorized
headlines.summary: Mexico's government stated that two US federal agents who died in a car crash were not permitted to participate in local operations. The agents reportedly worked for the CIA.

headlines.headline: Zelensky signs cooperation deals with Azerbaijan during first wartime visit
headlines.summary: Ukrainian president continues diplomatic push in the Caucasus region following similar deals with Gulf states.

perspectives.angle: Zelensky signs cooperation deals with Azerbaijan during first wartime visit
perspectives.summary: President Zelensky met with Azerbaijani President Aliyev in Baku, marking his first wartime visit to Azerbaijan and signing bilateral cooperation agreements.

perspectives.angle: Zelensky Signs Cooperation Deals During First Wartime Visit to Azerbaijan
perspectives.summary: President Volodymyr Zelensky arrived in Azerbaijan for talks with Azerbaijani President Ilham Aliyev, signing cooperation agreements in his first wartime visit to the Caucasus nation.

headlines.headline: US Southern Command creates new autonomous warfare unit
headlines.summary: New command to integrate autonomous, semi-autonomous and unmanned platforms across Latin American operations.

headlines.headline: Australia foreign minister to visit Japan, China, South Korea for energy security talks
headlines.summary: Australian Foreign Minister Penny Wong announced visits to Japan, China and South Korea this week for discussions on energy security amid disruptions caused by the Middle East conflict and Strait of Hormuz tensions.

perspectives.angle: Japan Energy Strategy Faces Competition From China in Central Asia
perspectives.summary: Japan efforts to secure energy resources in Central Asia and Latin America are encountering significant competition from China, which has already established a strong presence in these regions through infrastructure investments and trade agreements.

headlines.headline: Mali Defence Minister killed in al-Qaeda-linked attack near Bamako
headlines.summary: Mali Defence Minister Sadio Camara was killed in an attack by al Qaeda-linked group JNIM on his residence at the Kati military base outside Bamako, as coordinated assaults across Mali plunge the country into unprecedented security crisis.

perspectives.angle: Mali Defense Minister Killed in Coordinated Attack by Al-Qaeda and Tuareg Rebels
perspectives.summary: General Sadio Camara, a key figure in Malis military government, was killed during coordinated nationwide attacks by JNIM (al-Qaeda affiliate) and Tuareg fighters. The assault targeted multiple locations including the garrison town of Kati near the capital.

perspectives.angle: Mali Defence Minister Sadio Camara killed in coordinated attacks
perspectives.summary: Mali's Defence Minister Sadio Camara was killed in a coordinated attack on the garrison town of Kati, amid simultaneous attacks across the West African country, marking a major escalation in regional instability.

perspectives.angle: Al Qaeda-Linked Militants Launch Major Coordinated Attacks Across Mali
perspectives.summary: JNIM, an al Qaeda affiliate, and Tuareg rebels claimed responsibility for coordinated attacks across Mali targeting the capital Bamako and military bases. Experts called it one of the boldest operations in years.

headlines.headline: Malian troops and Russian mercenaries withdraw from Kidal after rebel offensive
headlines.summary: Malian troops and Russian Africa Corps mercenaries have withdrawn from the northern city of Kidal following coordinated attacks by Tuareg separatists and al-Qaeda-linked JNIM militants, marking a significant shift in the Sahel conflict.

headlines.headline: Egypt economic growth forecasts cut due to Iran war impact
headlines.summary: Analysts have reduced forecasts for Egyptian economic growth in a Reuters poll as the Iran conflict drives up energy prices and pressures inflation, with regional economic impacts expected to last months.

perspectives.angle: Egypt Economic Outlook Trimmed as Iran War Drives Up Energy Prices
perspectives.summary: Analysts reduced their forecasts for Egyptian economic growth as the Iran war drives up energy prices and puts pressure on inflation, according to a Reuters poll.

perspectives.angle: World's Largest Condom Maker Karex to Raise Prices Up to 30% Due to Iran War
perspectives.summary: Malaysia-based Karex, which produces over 5 billion condoms annually for brands like Durex and Trojan, announced price increases of up to 30% due to Iran war disruptions to oil-derived raw materials including ammonia and silicone lubricants.

perspectives.angle: Germany halves 2026 growth forecast as Iran war raises inflation
perspectives.summary: The German government has cut its 2026 growth forecast in half and raised inflation expectations, citing economic disruption from the Iran conflict and elevated energy costs.

perspectives.angle: Condom prices could surge 30% due to Iran war disruption
perspectives.summary: The world's largest condom producer warns that shortages of raw materials and chemicals caused by the Iran conflict could force price increases of up to 30 percent globally.

headlines.headline: Iran and US Stuck in Awkward "No War, No Peace" Limbo
headlines.summary: As diplomatic efforts stall with Trump canceling envoy visits to Pakistan, both Washington and Tehran are betting they can outlast the other in a precarious stalemate without a formal deal.

headlines.headline: France trains Ukrainian pilots on Mirage 2000-5F fighters under EU mission
headlines.summary: French Air Force pilots are training Ukrainian crews on Mirage 2000-5F aircraft within the EUMAM framework. The training includes combat operations and maintenance, marking a concrete contribution to European security.

headlines.headline: Trump Likely Target of White House Correspondents Dinner Shooting
headlines.summary: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche said President Trump and cabinet officials were likely the targets of a gunman who fired on security personnel at the annual White House Correspondents Association dinner. The suspect was taken into custody.

perspectives.angle: White House correspondents dinner gunman identified
perspectives.summary: The suspect arrested at the Washington Hilton event has been identified as Cole Tomas Allen, a 31-year-old from California. Trump and officials were evacuated after shots were fired outside the venue.

perspectives.angle: How the Shooting at White House Correspondents Dinner Unfolded
perspectives.summary: A gunman fired shots inside the hotel hosting the White House Correspondents Association dinner, causing President Trump and his cabinet to be evacuated. Security personnel engaged the suspect who was quickly apprehended.

perspectives.angle: White House Correspondents Dinner Shooting Live Updates
perspectives.summary: President Trump was safely evacuated from the White House Correspondents Association Dinner after shots were fired at the venue. The suspect is in custody and multiple officials provide ongoing updates.

perspectives.angle: Suspect in White House Correspondents Dinner Shooting Identified
perspectives.summary: The Secret Service identified the suspect as Cole Tomas Allen, armed with a shotgun. He fired on security agents guarding the event before being taken into custody. No injuries to the President or officials were reported.

perspectives.angle: Armed Man Storms White House Correspondents Dinner, Trump Evacuated Safely
perspectives.summary: A 31-year-old man armed with guns and knives charged the Washington Hilton ballroom during the White House Correspondents dinner. President Trump was safely evacuated and one Secret Service officer was injured. The suspect is in custody.

perspectives.angle: Trump evacuated from White House Correspondents Dinner after gunman breaches security
perspectives.summary: A 31-year-old California man armed with a shotgun, handgun and knives charged a Secret Service checkpoint at the Washington Hilton, exchanging gunfire with officers before being tackled. One Secret Service agent was injured but survived thanks to a bulletproof vest. Trump was safely evacuated and later addressed the nation from the White House.

perspectives.angle: CNN, NBC Report on White House Correspondents Dinner Shooting Incident
perspectives.summary: President Trump was evacuated from the White House Correspondents Dinner after shots were fired at the Washington Hilton. A suspect was taken into custody after exchanging gunfire with security. Trump and the First Lady were unharmed in the incident.

perspectives.angle: Trump Rushed Off Stage After Armed Man Opens Fire at White House Correspondents Dinner
perspectives.summary: An armed gunman stormed the Washington Hilton lobby as President Trump was beginning his speech at the annual White House Correspondents Association Dinner. Secret Service agents shot the suspect, who was armed with multiple guns and knives. One officer was hit but protected by a bulletproof vest.

perspectives.angle: Gunman opens fire at White House Correspondents Dinner, Trump evacuated safely
perspectives.summary: A shooter fired shots at the Washington Hilton during the White House Correspondents Association dinner, forcing President Trump and top officials to be evacuated. One officer was shot but saved by a bulletproof vest; the suspect is in custody.

perspectives.angle: Trump Evacuated from White House Correspondents Dinner After Gunshots
perspectives.summary: President Trump and First Lady Melania Trump were rushed out by Secret Service after a loud noise and shots fired at the Washington Hilton. A suspect is in custody and Trump later returned safely to the White House.

perspectives.angle: Trump evacuated from White House Correspondents Dinner after security incident, shooter reported dead
perspectives.summary: President Trump and First Lady Melania Trump were evacuated from the White House Correspondents Dinner after shots were fired outside the venue. The Secret Service neutralized the shooter, who was reported dead. Vice President Vance and several Cabinet secretaries were also evacuated.

perspectives.angle: Trump Evacuated from White House Correspondents Dinner After Shooting
perspectives.summary: President Donald Trump was evacuated by Secret Service after a shooter opened fire at the White House Correspondents Dinner in Washington. The shooter was killed by security forces. No injuries to Trump were reported.

headlines.headline: US-Iran Peace Hopes Fade as Trump Scraps Pakistan Talks
headlines.summary: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi returned to Pakistan for diplomatic efforts, but President Trump canceled envoy talks citing no progress. Both sides show little willingness to soften terms, leaving ceasefire prospects dim.

perspectives.angle: Trump cancels US envoys trip to Pakistan for Iran war talks
perspectives.summary: US President cancelled the delegation trip led by Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner after Iran said there were no plans for direct meeting. Peace prospects dim further.

perspectives.angle: US-Iran Peace Hopes Fade as Trump Cancels Negotiator Trip
perspectives.summary: President Trump canceled a planned trip by envoys Kushner and Witkoff to Pakistan for Iran ceasefire talks, citing inadequate Iranian proposals. Both sides show little willingness to soften terms as the war enters a stalemate phase.

perspectives.angle: US-Iran peace talks collapse as Trump cancels envoy visit to Pakistan
perspectives.summary: Diplomatic efforts stalled after Trump called off Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner trip to Islamabad, saying Iran offered a lot but not enough. Both sides show little willingness to soften terms as the war enters its third month.

perspectives.angle: US-Iran Ceasefire Talks Collapse as Trump Cancels Envoys Trip to Pakistan
perspectives.summary: The latest ceasefire negotiations between Washington and Tehran failed after Iran's foreign minister left Islamabad and Trump told his envoys not to travel. Trump said Iran needs to call if they want to talk, while Iran questioned how it can trust the US after its port blockade.

perspectives.angle: Trump Cancels US Peace Delegation Trip to Pakistan as Iran Talks Falter
perspectives.summary: President Trump canceled a planned US delegation trip to Pakistan for peace talks with Iran, citing "infighting" in Tehran and the lack of a clear negotiation plan. The move deals a blow to diplomatic efforts to end the US-Iran conflict.

perspectives.angle: US-Iran Peace Talks Stall as Trump Cancels Envoys Pakistan Trip
perspectives.summary: President Trump cancelled a planned trip by envoys Witkoff and Kushner to Pakistan for Iran negotiations, saying Iran improved its offer but not enough. Tehran says it will not be coerced into a deal through pressure or threats.

perspectives.angle: US-Iran Ceasefire Talks Collapse as Trump Cancels Envoy Trip to Pakistan
perspectives.summary: The latest US-Iran ceasefire talks appeared to fail before they began, with Iranian FM Araghchi leaving Pakistan and Trump saying he told envoys Witkoff and Kushner not to travel, citing too much time and work wasted on traveling.

perspectives.angle: Trump cancels envoys trip for Iran talks, says we have all the cards
perspectives.summary: President Trump scrapped a planned trip by Kushner and Witkoff to Pakistan for Iran negotiations, saying the talks involved too much travel and expense.

perspectives.angle: Trump Cancels US Envoys' Trip to Pakistan for Iran Talks
perspectives.summary: President Donald Trump cancelled a planned trip by US envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to Pakistan for Iran war mediation talks, saying 'If they want to talk, all they have to do is call.' Iran's foreign minister had already left Islamabad, dealing a setback to peace prospects.

perspectives.angle: Iran Rejects Direct Talks with US as Pakistan Mediation Continues
perspectives.summary: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi laid out Iran's demands in Islamabad while ruling out face-to-face meetings with US negotiators. The US has dispatched envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to Pakistan for indirect peace talks.

perspectives.angle: US envoys Witkoff and Kushner head to Pakistan for indirect Iran talks
perspectives.summary: Trump dispatched his special envoy and son-in-law to Islamabad to meet with Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi through Pakistani intermediaries, as Tehran rules out direct negotiations. The diplomatic push comes with three US aircraft carriers now positioned in the region.

perspectives.angle: Trump envoys head to Pakistan for Iran ceasefire talks
perspectives.summary: US special envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner are traveling to Pakistan as Iran signals willingness to make an offer satisfying US demands. Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi is already in Islamabad but no direct meeting with US delegation is confirmed.

headlines.headline: Israeli President Delays Decision on Netanyahu Pardon, Pushes for Plea Deal
headlines.summary: President Isaac Herzog said he will delay deciding on Prime Minister Netanyahus pardon request for corruption cases, urging that mediation efforts be exhausted first. The decision comes amid the ongoing Iran-Israel conflict.

perspectives.angle: Israel President Herzog seeks plea deal over pardon for Netanyahu
perspectives.summary: Israeli President Isaac Herzog has decided to shelve Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's pardon request, instead pushing for mediation that could lead to a plea deal in his corruption trial. Netanyahu faces charges in three cases.

headlines.headline: Israel sent Iron Dome and troops to UAE during Iran war
headlines.summary: Axios reports Israel deployed an Iron Dome battery and personnel to the UAE amid the Iran conflict, deepening defense ties between the countries.

perspectives.angle: Israel Waiting for Green Light to Renew War Against Iran
perspectives.summary: Defense Minister Katz says Israel is prepared to resume operations against Iran if the fragile ceasefire collapses, pending US approval.

headlines.headline: Mali Tuareg rebels announce Russian Africa Corps withdrawal from Kidal
headlines.summary: Tuareg rebels in northern Mali reached a deal for the Russian Africa Corps to withdraw from Kidal as fighting continues between the army and separatist forces.

headlines.headline: Russia warns months needed to rebalance oil market after Hormuz blockade
headlines.summary: Russian Deputy PM Alexander Novak warned of deep energy crisis as the Hormuz blockade prevents large volumes of oil from reaching global markets.

headlines.headline: China positioned to benefit from energy-driven renewables race
headlines.summary: The US-Iran war may have inadvertently triggered a global energy transition favoring Beijing's green technology leadership, analysts say.

headlines.headline: Car bomb explodes outside Belfast police station in suspected dissident attack
headlines.summary: A car bomb exploded outside a police station in Dunmurry, south Belfast, after a delivery vehicle was hijacked. Northern Ireland police called it a "miracle" no one was injured and suspect dissident republicans, possibly the New IRA.

perspectives.angle: Car Bomb Explodes Outside Northern Ireland Police Station
perspectives.summary: A car bomb exploded outside a police station in Dunmurry near Belfast after attackers placed an improvised device in a delivery drivers vehicle. Police condemned the attack as an attempt to undermine the Good Friday Agreement.

headlines.headline: Australia announces M armored vehicle deal amid weapons buildup
headlines.summary: Australia will spend more than $800 million on armored vehicles, adding to its recent weapons purchases as the Middle East conflict drives concerns about regional security and supply chain vulnerabilities.

headlines.headline: Iran FM Araqchi discusses Hormuz security and war end efforts with Oman
headlines.summary: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi held talks in Oman on Sunday to discuss security in the Strait of Hormuz and broader Gulf waters, as well as diplomatic efforts to end the Iran-U.S. conflict. The meeting comes as US-Iran peace talks remain stalled.

headlines.headline: King Charles state visit under security review after Washington shooting
headlines.summary: Buckingham Palace is holding discussions with US authorities about whether the shooting at the White House Correspondents' Dinner will affect security planning for King Charles's state visit to the US this week. The King expressed relief that Trump was unharmed.

headlines.headline: Sixteen killed in Russia-Ukraine strikes on Chernobyl anniversary
headlines.summary: At least 16 people killed in strikes across Ukraine and Russia as the 40th anniversary of the Chernobyl disaster highlighted nuclear risks. Ukraine struck a major oil refinery in Yaroslavl deep inside Russia. Zelenskyy warned Russia is bringing world to brink of man-made disaster.

perspectives.angle: At least 16 killed in Ukraine-Russia strikes on Chernobyl anniversary
perspectives.summary: Strikes across Ukraine, Russian-occupied Crimea, and inside Russia killed at least 16 people as the 40th anniversary of the Chernobyl nuclear disaster prompted fresh warnings about risks from attacks near nuclear facilities. Ukrainian forces struck an oil refinery deep inside Russian territory.

perspectives.angle: Seven Killed as Russia Launches Massive Overnight Strike on Ukraine
perspectives.summary: Russian missiles and drones killed at least seven people across Ukraine overnight, including five in Dnipro where an apartment building was hit. Ukraine reported repelling most of over 600 drones in one of the largest attacks in days. Romania investigated an object falling on its territory near the Ukrainian border.

headlines.headline: China backs Myanmar's push to ease diplomatic isolation, fight telecoms scams
headlines.summary: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi concluded a five-day Southeast Asia tour by backing Myanmar's efforts to reduce its diplomatic isolation and crack down on telecoms fraud operations.

headlines.headline: Two US citizens among 19 killed in Philippines clashes with communist rebels
headlines.summary: Two American citizens were among 19 suspected New People's Army guerrillas killed during clashes with Philippine military forces on April 19 in Negros Occidental province.

headlines.headline: EU commissioners arrive in Kyiv to secure Chornobyl site
headlines.summary: EU Economy Commissioner Valdis Dombrovskis and Energy Commissioner Dan Jørgensen visited Kyiv on April 26 to discuss making the Chornobyl nuclear site safe again, marking continued European support for Ukraine's nuclear safety.

headlines.headline: Syria holds first public trial of Assad-era officials in Damascus
headlines.summary: Syria opened its first public trial of officials linked to former President Bashar Assad's rule in Damascus on Sunday, marking a significant step in post-civil war accountability efforts.

headlines.headline: Mexico: US agents killed in crash not authorized for local operations
headlines.summary: Two US federal agents, reportedly CIA, died in a car crash after a Mexican-led operation to destroy a drug lab. Mexican authorities say they were not permitted to operate in the country.

perspectives.angle: Mexico Says US Agents Killed in Crash Weren't Authorized to Operate There
perspectives.summary: The Mexican government stated that two US federal agents - widely reported to be CIA officers - killed in a car crash during an anti-narcotics raid were not authorized to operate on Mexican territory. One had entered as a visitor, the other on a diplomatic passport.

headlines.headline: Palestinians hold local elections in West Bank and Gaza
headlines.summary: Local elections conducted in the occupied West Bank and one Gazan city, though Hamas and other groups did not participate.

perspectives.angle: Palestinians vote in local elections across West Bank and Gaza
perspectives.summary: Local elections held in the occupied West Bank and one Gazan city, though Hamas and other groups are not participating in what marks a rare electoral moment.

perspectives.angle: Gaza to hold first local election in two decades
perspectives.summary: Residents of Deir al-Balah are set to vote in municipal elections, the first in Gaza in 20 years. Hamas is not participating in the elections scheduled for this weekend.

perspectives.angle: Gaza Set for First Local Elections in Two Decades
perspectives.summary: Gaza is preparing to hold its first local elections in nearly 20 years. Hamas is not participating in the municipal elections in Deir al-Balah scheduled for this weekend.

headlines.headline: Afghanistan urges Afghans stuck in Qatar to return home
headlines.summary: Taliban government calls on Afghans who helped US forces and are now stranded in Qatar to return to Afghanistan.

headlines.headline: Venezuela prisoner release scheme nearing end, rights groups warn
headlines.summary: More than 500 political prisoners remain jailed as amnesty program winds down amid criticism from human rights organizations.

headlines.headline: Germany Suspects Russia Behind Signal Phishing Campaign Targeting Politicians
headlines.summary: The German government believes Russia orchestrated phishing attacks via the Signal messaging app targeting high-profile politicians, diplomats, military officers, and journalists. At least 300 accounts were compromised in the cyber campaign.

perspectives.angle: Germany attributes global Signal and WhatsApp cyber campaign to Russia
perspectives.summary: Berlin says the coordinated disinformation attack on encrypted messaging platforms likely originated in Moscow.

perspectives.angle: Germany Blames Russia for Global Cyber Campaign Targeting Signal and WhatsApp
perspectives.summary: Berlin believes Russia is behind a global cyber operation targeting encrypted messaging apps Signal and WhatsApp, according to Der Spiegel, marking an escalation in Russian cyber warfare tactics.

perspectives.angle: Germany attributes global Signal and WhatsApp cyber campaign to Russia
perspectives.summary: Berlin believes the coordinated attack on encrypted messaging platforms presumably originated in Russia, according to Der Spiegel, highlighting ongoing hybrid warfare concerns.

headlines.headline: Colombia Bus Bombing Kills 13 Ahead of Presidential Election
headlines.summary: At least 13 people were killed and 17 wounded in an explosives attack on a bus in western Colombia. Authorities blamed dissidents of the FARC guerrilla group for the violence amid escalating pre-election tensions.

perspectives.angle: Colombia bus bombing kills 13 amid escalating drug-trafficking violence
perspectives.summary: An explosive device detonated on a bus in Cauca province, killing 13 and injuring 38 including five children. Army chief called it a terrorist act blamed on FARC dissidents, amid a wave of 26 similar incidents in the last two days.

perspectives.angle: Colombia Highway Bombing Kills 14 Ahead of Presidential Election
perspectives.summary: A bomb attack on the Pan-American Highway in Cauca department killed 14 and injured 38, including five minors. Authorities blamed FARC dissidents led by Ivan Mordisco, the country's most-wanted criminal, as political violence escalates before the May 31 vote.

perspectives.angle: Colombia Highway Bombing Kills 14 as President Blames Rebel Dissidents
perspectives.summary: A bombing on a Colombian highway killed at least 14 people. President Gustavo Petro blamed the attack on rebels linked to dissident FARC factions. The attacks come one month before the presidential election.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian Drone Strikes Apartment Building in Yekaterinburg
headlines.summary: A Ukrainian drone hit a multi-story apartment building in Russia's fourth-largest city of Yekaterinburg, marking the first attack on the city since the conflict began. Regional authorities reported damage to residential infrastructure.

headlines.headline: Finland Joins Nordic Boycott Over Russian Athlete Decision
headlines.summary: Finland announced it will not host international championships while World Aquatics allows Russian and Belarusian athletes to compete, joining Norway and Poland in protest against the governing body's policy.

perspectives.angle: Finland Joins Nordic Boycott of World Aquatics Over Russia Participation
perspectives.summary: Finland announced it will not host international swimming championships while World Aquatics allows Russian and Belarusian athletes to compete, joining Norway and Poland in a growing Nordic protest against the governing body.

headlines.headline: Ukraine unveils new AI-powered strike drones Anubis and Seth-X for deep missions
headlines.summary: Ukrainian firm Airlogix revealed two new drones: Anubis for deep-strike missions with stealth characteristics, and Seth-X designed for swarm attacks, expanding Ukraine's AI strike capabilities in contested environments.

headlines.headline: Chinese analysts study how Iran detected and damaged US F-35 stealth jet
headlines.summary: South China Morning Post reports that Chinese military analysts are mapping out how Iran managed to detect and damage a US F-35 stealth fighter during combat operations, suggesting electro-optical/infrared sensor systems may have been used.

headlines.headline: Dutch intelligence: Russia could fight NATO within a year after Ukraine war ends
headlines.summary: The Dutch MIVD warns Russia could be ready for a regional conflict with NATO within a year after hostilities in Ukraine cease, aiming to create political division in the alliance through limited territorial gains under nuclear threats.

headlines.headline: Thailand and Cambodia sign ceasefire with Trump presiding at ASEAN summit
headlines.summary: The two nations signed an expanded ceasefire deal ending their five-day war, with Trump taking credit for pushing negotiations. Thailand will release prisoners and Cambodia will withdraw heavy artillery as first steps.

headlines.headline: Russian Defense Minister Visits North Korea as Military Ties Deepen
headlines.summary: Russian Defense Minister Andrei Belousov arrived in North Korea for talks with top leadership, the second senior Russian official to visit Pyongyang this week. The visit follows State Duma Chairman Vyacheslav Volodin meeting with Kim Jong Un to commemorate North Korean troop deployment in Ukraine.

headlines.headline: TotalEnergies Warns of Global Energy Shortages as Hormuz Crisis Continues
headlines.summary: The head of TotalEnergies warned of global energy shortages if the Iran war continues for months, as French President Macron reaffirmed efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. LNG markets are bracing for sustained price elevation throughout 2026.

perspectives.angle: Macron Pushes to Reopen Hormuz as TotalEnergies Warns of Global Energy Shortages
perspectives.summary: French President Macron reiterated efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz after TotalEnergies CEO warned of global energy shortages if the Iran war continues for months. The waterway remains nearly blocked, disrupting oil and LNG shipments worldwide.

perspectives.angle: Macron reaffirms efforts to reopen Strait of Hormuz as TotalEnergies warns of shortages
perspectives.summary: The French president said he is focused on reopening the Strait of Hormuz after TotalEnergies CEO warned of global energy shortages if the Iran war continues for months.

perspectives.angle: Macron Reaffirms Efforts to Reopen Strait of Hormuz as TotalEnergies Warns of Energy Shortages
perspectives.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron reiterated his focus on efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a day after TotalEnergies CEO warned of global energy shortages if the Iran war continues for months.

perspectives.angle: Macron Pushes to Reopen Strait of Hormuz as TotalEnergies Warns of Shortages
perspectives.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron reaffirmed efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz amid the Iran conflict. TotalEnergies CEO warned of global energy shortages if the war continues for months.

perspectives.angle: Sweden warns of fuel rationing as Hormuz standoff disrupts global energy flows
perspectives.summary: Rising oil and gas prices linked to the Strait of Hormuz standoff are pushing Sweden to prepare for tougher economic measures as inflation risks grow. The government is warning citizens that fuel rationing may be necessary.

headlines.headline: France and Greece Strengthen Defense Alliance Amid NATO Uncertainty
headlines.summary: French President Macron and Greek PM Mitsotakis signed expanded bilateral defense agreements in Athens, affirming that EU defense capabilities complement rather than replace NATO. The Greece-France Alliance 2.0 comes as concerns grow over US commitment to the alliance.

perspectives.angle: Macron says EU mutual defense clause is stronger than NATO amid US commitment doubts
perspectives.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron declared the EUs Article 42.7 mutual defense clause stronger than NATO, as European leaders debate building independent defense capabilities. The statement reflects growing anxiety about Washingtons reliability.

perspectives.angle: France and Greece Say EU Defense Buildup No Alternative to NATO
perspectives.summary: President Macron stated the EU push for defensive capabilities is not meant to create a NATO alternative but to address a long-standing US request for Europe to do more, amid transatlantic tensions over the Iran war.

perspectives.angle: Former NATO Chief Urges Britain and France to Lead New European Defense
perspectives.summary: A former NATO secretary general called for Britain and France to lead a new European security arrangement, warning the continent must be prepared to act without US support as transatlantic relations strain over the Iran conflict.

perspectives.angle: EU Leaders Eye Mutual Defense Pact Amid Trump NATO Uncertainty
perspectives.summary: European Union nations are examining a little-known mutual assistance obligation as an alternative to NATO, as Trump continues to criticize the alliance and European defense commitments.

perspectives.angle: EU Leaders Push Mutual Defense Pact Amid NATO Crisis
perspectives.summary: European Union members are planning how to come to each others aid in the event of an attack as NATO faces its worst crisis in history due to Trump criticism.

headlines.headline: Major Coordinated Attack Hits Multiple Cities Across Mali
headlines.summary: Jihadist group JNIM and separatist Azawad Liberation Front launched simultaneous attacks on Bamako, Kati, Gao, Kidal, and other cities in what analysts call the largest coordinated jihadist attack in Mali in years. Gunmen attacked the airport area and a military helicopter was reportedly shot down near Gao.

headlines.headline: Putin Approval Ratings Drop as Russian Influencers Voice Growing Discontent
headlines.summary: A viral 19-minute video by a Russian blogger with 31 million views has sparked a wave of public criticism over internet restrictions and the strained wartime economy. Putin's approval fell to 65.6% in state polls, the lowest since before the Ukraine war began, amid growing frustration with the conflict's fifth year.

headlines.headline: Japanese Destroyer Transits Taiwan Strait in First Major Passage Under Takaichi
headlines.summary: The JS Ikazuchi, a Murasame-class destroyer, conducted a deliberate 14-hour transit through the Taiwan Strait, sparking outrage in Beijing. The move signals a significant shift in Japan's regional security stance under Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi.

headlines.headline: Macron Warns Europe Must Wake Up as US, Russia, and China Align Against It
headlines.summary: Speaking in Athens, the French president called for European strategic autonomy, saying Washington, Moscow, and Beijing are now categorically opposed to European interests. The remarks come as Germany unveils its first comprehensive defense strategy since WWII.

headlines.headline: Macron Affirms EU Defense Clause Stronger Than NATO's Article 5
headlines.summary: The French president said during a visit to Greece that the EU's mutual defense clause (Article 42.7) is 'stronger' than NATO's collective defense provision, citing military aid sent to Cyprus after a drone attack. The statement comes amid uncertainty over US commitment to NATO.

perspectives.angle: Macron: EU Joint Defense Clause Is Stronger Than NATOs Article 5
perspectives.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron stated that the EU mutual defense clause (Article 42.7) is steadier than NATOs Article 5, pushing for greater European strategic autonomy amid questions about US reliability.

perspectives.angle: Macron says EU defence clause stronger than NATO Article 5
perspectives.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron stated that the EU mutual assistance clause was "stronger" than NATO Article 5, citing recent military aid to Cyprus as proof of Europe ability to defend itself amid concerns over US reliability.

perspectives.angle: Macron says EU defense clause is stronger than NATO Article 5
perspectives.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron declared during a visit to Greece that the EU mutual defense clause (Article 42.7) is stronger than NATO Article 5 because it leaves no option for non-compliance. The statement comes as Trump questions NATO and US commitment to European defense.

headlines.headline: Ukrainians Mark Chernobyl Anniversary Amid War Fears
headlines.summary: Residents of Slavutych gathered for a midnight vigil commemorating the 40th anniversary of the Chernobyl nuclear disaster, defying wartime curfews. The anniversary comes as Ukraine worries Russia's war could trigger another nuclear catastrophe.

headlines.headline: Tunisia suspends one of Africas oldest human rights groups in widening crackdown
headlines.summary: Authorities ordered a one-month suspension of the Tunisian League for Human Rights, part of the Nobel Peace Prize-winning National Dialogue Quartet. The move signals deepening authoritarianism in the North African nation.

headlines.headline: Chinas top diplomat meets Myanmar junta leader on regional tour
headlines.summary: Chinas Foreign Minister visited Naypyidaw and met with the military-backed government leader, deepening Beijings regional ties as Myanmars civil war continues. The visit underscores Chinas growing influence in Southeast Asia.

perspectives.angle: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi Meets Myanmar's Military Leader
perspectives.summary: China's top diplomat visited Myanmar's capital Naypyitaw and met with Min Aung Hlaing as part of a regional tour aimed at strengthening Beijing's political, security and strategic ties in Southeast Asia. The visit follows Min Aung Hlaing's swearing in as president on April 10.

headlines.headline: Hungary's Orban Refuses Parliament Seat After Historic Election Defeat
headlines.summary: Viktor Orban, Hungary's dominant political figure for 36 years, said he will not take up his parliament seat following his party's landslide defeat. He plans to lead Fidesz through a "renewal" process, with analysts seeing his loss as evidence that right-wing populism may be peaking.

perspectives.angle: Hungary's Orban to Leave Parliament After Landslide Election Defeat
perspectives.summary: Viktor Orban announced he will give up his parliamentary mandate after losing to Peter Magyar in Hungary's election. The long-serving prime minister said he wants to stay on as Fidesz party leader while rebuilding the national side.

headlines.headline: India Signs $1.2 Billion Deal for Russian Long-Range Air-to-Air Missiles
headlines.summary: India has reportedly signed a deal for 300 Russian R-37M ultra-long-range missiles designed to target support aircraft like airborne early-warning and tanker planes. The move comes as Pakistan and China continue integrating their aerial weapons systems.

headlines.headline: Trump Cancels Kushner and Witkoff Iran Mission After Tehran Offer Falls Short
headlines.summary: President Trump cancelled his envoys' trip to Pakistan for Iran talks, saying Tehran 'offered a lot but not enough.' Iran had said no direct talks were planned and its foreign minister left Islamabad for Oman before heading to Russia.

perspectives.angle: Trump Cancels Envoys Trip for Iran Talks, Says We Have All the Cards
perspectives.summary: President Trump called off the trip of US envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to Pakistan for Iran negotiations, saying too much time has been wasted on traveling.

headlines.headline: Republicans Recalibrate Midterm Strategy Amid Trump Approval Slump and Iran War
headlines.summary: With gas prices up, Trump's approval ratings down, and the Iran war dragging on, Republicans are retooling their midterm election blueprint to focus on Trump's policies while distancing themselves from the president personally.

perspectives.angle: Republicans Retool Midterm Strategy Amid Iran War and Gas Prices
perspectives.summary: With US gas prices up and President Trump's approval ratings down as the Iran war drags on, Republicans are recalibrating their blueprint for November's midterm elections, focusing on Trump's policies while distancing from Trump himself.

headlines.headline: Trump Fires Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem
headlines.summary: President Trump fired Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem following mounting criticism over her handling of immigration enforcement and disaster response. Senator Markwayne Mullin has been nominated as her replacement.

headlines.headline: Trump Vows No Hormuz Blockade Lift Until Iran Deal Reached
headlines.summary: President Trump stated the US will not lift its naval blockade of Iranian ports until a deal is reached with Tehran. US Central Command reports 27 vessels have been turned away since the blockade began, while Iran maintains its own blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: Acid Attacks Target Indonesian Activists Amid Growing Climate of Repression
headlines.summary: Environmental and human rights activists in Indonesia face escalating attacks, including acid assaults, under President Prabowo's government which rights groups say is increasingly hostile to dissent.

headlines.headline: EU Seeks Alternative Middle East Energy Routes to Bypass Hormuz
headlines.summary: European Commission President von der Leyen announces plans to work with Gulf countries on new export infrastructure that avoids the Strait of Hormuz, as the bloc's energy bill has skyrocketed by €25 billion due to the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Former MIT President Warns US Is Losing Innovation Race to China
headlines.summary: L. Rafael Reif, who led MIT from 2012 to 2022, argues in Foreign Affairs that America is ceding scientific leadership to China, pointing to advances in EVs, nuclear energy, and military technology.

headlines.headline: Lebanese Civilians Question Fragile Ceasefire as Violence Simmers in South
headlines.summary: Residents in southern Lebanon express skepticism about the extended ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah, as continuing attacks and tensions threaten to reignite the conflict.

headlines.headline: Germany Readies Naval Units for Possible Hormuz Deployment
headlines.summary: Germany is sending naval units to the Mediterranean in preparation for possible deployment in an international mission to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, which remains blocked by Iran.

perspectives.angle: Germany deploys minesweeper to Mediterranean for possible Strait of Hormuz mission
perspectives.summary: Germany's defense minister announced the deployment of a minesweeper to the Mediterranean Sea in preparation for a potential mission to the Strait of Hormuz, as European nations consider naval operations to secure the vital oil transit route.

perspectives.angle: Germany Deploying Warships to Mediterranean for Possible Hormuz Mission
perspectives.summary: German navy vessels are being sent to the Mediterranean as Berlin considers a potential role in protecting shipping through the Strait of Hormuz amid Iran attacks on commercial vessels.

headlines.headline: Zelenskyy Signs Military-Industrial Agreement With Azerbaijan as Russian Attacks Kill 10
headlines.summary: Ukraine's president signed a military-industrial cooperation agreement in Baku as Russia continued missile and drone strikes on Ukrainian cities. The death toll from recent attacks rose to at least 10.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian drones strike Russian oil refinery in Yaroslavl, hit multiple regions including occupied Crimea
headlines.summary: A fire erupted at an oil refinery in the Russian city of Yaroslavl as Ukrainian drones attacked multiple Russian regions overnight. Strikes also targeted occupied Crimea, marking an escalation in Ukraine's long-range drone campaign against Russian energy infrastructure.

perspectives.angle: Ukrainian Drones Strike Russian Refinery and Chemical Plant in Deep-Raids
perspectives.summary: Ukrainian long-range drones hit the Slavneft-YANOS refinery in Yaroslavl and a chemical complex in Volgograd, continuing Kyiv's campaign to degrade Russian industrial infrastructure. Five workers were hospitalized with chemical burns at the Apatit nitrogen complex.

headlines.headline: South Korea and Japan face renewed nuclear deterrent debate as Iran war reshapes security calculations
headlines.summary: Seven in ten South Koreans now support acquiring nuclear weapons, while Japan's PM refuses to rule out the option. The Iran war has brought the long-simmering debate over nuclear proliferation in US allies to a boil, with IAEA sounding the alarm.

headlines.headline: Russian forces pound Ukraine with massive drone and missile barrage
headlines.summary: Russian forces attacked southeastern Ukraine including Dnipro with waves of drones and missiles, killing at least 10 people and injuring dozens in one of the largest attacks of the war.

perspectives.angle: Russian Drone Strikes Kill 5 in Dnipro as Ukraine War Enters Fifth Year
perspectives.summary: Russian drone and missile attacks killed at least 5 people and wounded 46 in Dnipro. The strikes destroyed residential buildings and businesses. Romania reported drone fragments on its territory near the Ukraine border.

perspectives.angle: Russian Strikes on Dnipro Kill Five, Wound Dozens
perspectives.summary: Russian drone and missile strikes on the Ukrainian city of Dnipro killed at least five people and wounded 46, authorities said. The attacks caused fires across the city and partially destroyed several apartment buildings.

headlines.headline: Al Qaeda affiliate launches coordinated attacks across Mali
headlines.summary: An al Qaeda affiliate and Tuareg rebels claimed coordinated attacks on army bases across Mali in one of the boldest insurgent operations against the military-led government.

perspectives.angle: Mali separatists join al-Qaida-linked militants in coordinated attacks on army
perspectives.summary: Tuareg-led Azawad Liberation Front said it partnered with JNIM to launch one of the biggest coordinated attacks on Malian forces in Bamako and four other cities. At least 16 wounded. First time separatists explicitly confirmed alliance with jihadists.

perspectives.angle: Mali Declares Curfew After Coordinated Terrorist Attacks Hit Bamako
perspectives.summary: Authorities imposed a night curfew in the Gao region after coordinated attacks by terrorist groups targeted military sites in Bamako and other areas. The military claims hundreds of attackers were neutralized.

perspectives.angle: Islamic Militants and Separatists Claim Sweeping Attacks Across Mali
perspectives.summary: Gunfire was heard near Malis international airport in the capital Bamako early Saturday. Coordinated attacks by gunmen targeted military positions across the West African nation.

perspectives.angle: Mali Hit by Wave of Coordinated Attacks from Armed Groups
perspectives.summary: Gunfire and explosions rocked Mali capital Bamako and other cities in one of the most significant coordinated attacks in years, as jihadist insurgents and separatist rebels exploit worsening insecurity in the Sahel.

perspectives.angle: Coordinated Attacks Hit Multiple Cities Across Mali
perspectives.summary: Gunmen launched coordinated attacks against Malian military positions in the capital Bamako, Kati, Sevare, and northern areas in one of the largest assaults on the junta in years. The attacks targeted the international airport and military bases.

perspectives.angle: Armed groups launch coordinated attacks across Mali
perspectives.summary: Explosions and gunfire reported in Bamako, Kati, Gao, Kidal, and Sevare as jihadist group JNIM and separatist Azawad Liberation Front stage simultaneous assaults. The UK Foreign Office has advised against all travel to Mali, and Bamako International Airport has been temporarily closed.

perspectives.angle: Coordinated jihadist attacks hit multiple cities across Mali
perspectives.summary: Explosions and gunfire reported in Bamako, Gao, Kidal and Sevare in what analysts describe as the largest coordinated jihadist attack on Mali in years. Airport closed as military battles JNIM and Tuareg rebels.

perspectives.angle: Mali Army Reports Coordinated Armed Group Attacks Across Country
perspectives.summary: Mali's military says armed groups have launched coordinated attacks across the country, with witnesses reporting explosions and sustained gunfire near a military base outside the capital, Bamako.

headlines.headline: Washingtons Resentment Toward Paper Tiger NATO Growing
headlines.summary: The Trump administration has accused NATO allies of failing to show solidarity during the Iran war, with frustration particularly strong toward Spain, France, and the United Kingdom over their perceived lack of support.

headlines.headline: Japans Accelerated Remilitarization Raises Regional Concerns
headlines.summary: Japans quickened remilitarization under the Takaichi administration, including eased restrictions on exporting lethal weapons, is posing grave threats to neighbors and requires international vigilance, analysts warn.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Exports Drone Warfare Expertise to Allies
headlines.summary: Ukraines hard-won drone warfare knowledge is being shared with allies, as three Ukrainian drone operators reveal how the country has become a global leader in unmanned aerial warfare technology.

headlines.headline: Colombia explosives attack kills at least 13
headlines.summary: An explosives attack in western Colombia killed at least 13 people and wounded 17 others, in one of the deadliest incidents amid escalating violence by FARC dissident groups.

perspectives.angle: Colombia Highway Bomb Attack Kills 10 Ahead of Presidential Election
perspectives.summary: A highway bombing in the Cauca department killed 10 people and injured at least 12, with authorities blaming FARC dissidents. The attack comes amid a wave of violence before next month's presidential election.

headlines.headline: Russia parliament speaker visits Pyongyang for war memorial ceremony
headlines.summary: Russian State Duma Chairman Vyacheslav Volodin arrived in North Korea to attend the opening of a memorial for North Korean troops who died fighting in Ukraine, highlighting deepening military ties between Moscow and Pyongyang.

headlines.headline: China and Mozambique to map critical minerals in conflict zone
headlines.summary: China and Mozambique agreed to survey rare earth elements, graphite, and lithium deposits in insurgency-hit Cabo Delgado province, as Beijing seeks to secure critical mineral supplies for the green energy transition.

headlines.headline: Iran Resumes Commercial Flights From Tehran Airport
headlines.summary: Iran has resumed commercial flights from Tehran Imam Khomeini International Airport for the first time since the US-Israel conflict began two months ago, with flights operating to Istanbul, Muscat, Medina, and domestic destinations including Mashhad.

headlines.headline: Macron warns US not "fully reliable ally," urges European self-reliance
headlines.summary: French President Macron said the US can no longer be seen as a fully reliable ally during a visit to Athens, calling for greater European autonomy in defense. The remarks came after a Pentagon memo revealed US plans to suspend Spain's NATO membership over its refusal to support the Iran war.

headlines.headline: Saudi Arabia looks to China and regional partners as US security guarantee wavers
headlines.summary: The Iran war has exposed vulnerabilities in US security guarantees to Gulf allies, prompting Saudi Arabia to diversify partnerships with China, Pakistan, and Turkey. Riyadh has signed defense deals with Beijing and a mutual defense pact with nuclear-armed Pakistan.

headlines.headline: China Warns US Export Bills Risk Disrupting Global Chip Supply Chains
headlines.summary: China said it is closely monitoring US legislative plans after the House Foreign Affairs Committee advanced export-control bills targeting semiconductors, warning of potential global supply chain disruptions.

headlines.headline: Mexico Says US Agents Killed in Crash Were Not Authorized for Local Operations
headlines.summary: Mexicos government stated that two US federal agents killed in a car crash in the northern region were not authorized to participate in any local operation.

headlines.headline: China Drops Hint Its Next Aircraft Carrier May Be Nuclear-Powered
headlines.summary: A video released for the PLA Navys anniversary uses wordplay suggesting a new nuclear-powered warship is under construction, potentially reshaping the Pacific naval balance.

headlines.headline: Pakistan and Iran Leaders Discuss Regional Peace in Phone Call
headlines.summary: Pakistani PM Shehbaz Sharif spoke with Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian about regional stability as diplomatic efforts continue to resolve tensions with the US. The call comes amid uncertainty over whether US envoys will travel to Islamabad for talks.

headlines.headline: Iran FM Travels to Oman After Sharing Diplomatic Framework in Pakistan
headlines.summary: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi arrived in Oman from Islamabad where he presented a framework for ending the US-Iran conflict. Araghchi expressed skepticism about US commitment to diplomacy as Trump cancels envoy visit.

headlines.headline: Trump Renews Call to End Filibuster Over SAVE America Act Impasse
headlines.summary: President Trump called for eliminating the filibuster to pass the Safeguard American Voter Eligibility Act, which remains stalled in the Senate. The push highlights ongoing legislative gridlock on election-related legislation.

headlines.headline: US eases sanctions to let Venezuela pay Maduro legal fees
headlines.summary: The US Department of Justice modified sanctions to allow the Venezuelan government to cover legal fees for former president Nicolas Maduro, who faces federal drug trafficking charges in New York. Maduro defense lawyers had argued that blocking payment violated his right to counsel.

headlines.headline: US senator urges China participation in nuclear arms control
headlines.summary: Senator Deb Fischer, chair of the Senate Armed Services strategic forces subcommittee, said China must be involved in any nuclear arms control talks. China has repeatedly refused to join US-Russia nuclear agreements, seeking parity before negotiations.

headlines.headline: Gaza's Deir el-Balah Holds First Municipal Elections in 20 Years
headlines.summary: Residents of Deir el-Balah in central Gaza cast ballots in the first municipal elections since 2006, with voters expressing joy at participating in democratic processes after two decades. The elections come amid stalled ceasefire negotiations.

headlines.headline: Islamabad bears the cost of Pakistan mediates US-Iran negotiations
headlines.summary: Pakistani capital experiences semi-lockdown conditions as the country attempts to broker peace between Washington and Tehran. Next round of talks remains uncertain as daily life disrupted.

perspectives.angle: Islamabad Residents Bear Cost of Pakistan US-Iran Mediation
perspectives.summary: As Pakistan hosts US-Iran talks, local residents face disruptions from a semi-lockdown in the capital. The mediation effort comes with significant costs to daily life and commerce in Islamabad.

headlines.headline: China tungsten export restrictions hit global hobby and defense supply chains
headlines.summary: Beijing limits exports of tungsten, a critical material for both hobby manufacturing and defense industries. The restrictions come amid broader trade tensions and supply chain realignments.

headlines.headline: Low Earth orbit control could determine future world order, Chinese analysis warns
headlines.summary: A Chinese strategic analysis highlights Starlink capabilities in conflict zones and warns of a new form of power that could allow or cut connectivity in real time, reshaping geopolitical leverage.

headlines.headline: King Charles III to Visit US Amid Iran War Tensions
headlines.summary: King Charles III and Queen Camilla begin a four-day state visit to the US on Monday, seeking to strengthen US-UK ties despite tensions over Britain refusing to support the Iran war. The visit follows Queen Elizabeth IIs 1991 precedent of addressing Congress.

perspectives.angle: UN Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty Review Opens Amid Global War Tensions
perspectives.summary: Signatories to the landmark NPT meet at the UN as arms control agreements between major powers have collapsed and nuclear capabilities expand. The nine nuclear-armed states now possess over 12,000 warheads, with the US and Russia holding 90%.

headlines.headline: Major Russian Attack Kills Seven in Ukraine as Dnipro Struck by Barrage
headlines.summary: Russia launched over 660 drones and missiles in a massive overnight attack that killed at least seven people and wounded dozens in Dnipro and other Ukrainian regions. It marks one of the largest Russian barrages in recent weeks.

perspectives.angle: Seven killed in major Russian attack on Ukraine
perspectives.summary: A major Russian strike on the city of Dnipro killed at least seven people, with officials saying four died in a strike on a residential building.

perspectives.angle: 8 killed, 21 injured in Russian attacks on Ukraine over past day
perspectives.summary: Russian forces continue to pound Ukrainian cities with drone and missile strikes as the front-line war grinds on.

perspectives.angle: Seven killed in major Russian attack on Ukraine as jets scrambled in Romania
perspectives.summary: Russia launched its largest attack in days with over 600 drones and missiles, killing at least seven people including five in Dnipro where an apartment building was hit. British Typhoons were scrambled from Romania as Russian drones approached NATO airspace.

headlines.headline: Four Killed in Israeli Strikes on Southern Lebanon Despite Ceasefire
headlines.summary: Israeli strikes killed four people in southern Lebanon, state media reported, while the Israeli military said Hezbollah had fired rockets at Israel. The attacks come despite an extended ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon.

perspectives.angle: Israeli strikes kill six in southern Lebanon despite ceasefire agreement
perspectives.summary: Israeli airstrikes killed six people in southern Lebanon, according to the Lebanese health ministry. The strikes come amid accusations of Hezbollah ceasefire violations and rising tensions along the Israel-Lebanon border.

headlines.headline: Ukraine and Russia Swap 193 Prisoners Each in UAE-Facilitated Exchange
headlines.summary: Russia and Ukraine exchanged 193 prisoners of war each in a swap facilitated by the United States and UAE. The returned prisoners included soldiers, border guards, and police, some with injuries.

perspectives.angle: Russia-Ukraine Exchange Deadly Attacks as Zelenskyy Calls for More Talks
perspectives.summary: Russian drone and missile strikes killed at least 3 and wounded 21 in Dnipro overnight. Both sides reported casualties in intensified fighting as Zelenskyy pushed for continued diplomatic engagement.

headlines.headline: HH-60W Jolly Green II helicopters to take on doomsday evacuation role in Washington
headlines.summary: The US Air Force will modify its new combat rescue helicopters for VIP airlift and continuity of government operations, replacing aging UH-1N Twin Hueys.

headlines.headline: Navy rushing Hellfire missiles to carrier strike groups for counter-drone ops
headlines.summary: The US Navy is pushing more hard-kill counter-drone capabilities to its fleet as it confronts growing threats from Iranian one-way attack drones in the Middle East.

perspectives.angle: Navy Rushing Hellfire Missiles to Carrier Strike Groups for Drone Defense
perspectives.summary: The U.S. Navy is accelerating deployment of Hellfire missiles to carrier strike groups as counter-drone capabilities amid growing threats from one-way attack drones in the Middle East theater.

perspectives.angle: Navy rushing Hellfire missiles to carrier groups for anti-drone defense
perspectives.summary: The US Navy is accelerating deployment of Hellfire missiles to carrier strike groups as the threat of one-way attack drones in the Middle East intensifies following Iranian attacks on shipping.

headlines.headline: Swedish intelligence warns Russian Orthodox church is espionage platform
headlines.summary: A Russian Orthodox church near Vasteras airport with an unusually tall spire and elusive priest is suspected of being used by Moscow for espionage and hybrid activities threatening Sweden's security.

perspectives.angle: Russia suspected of using Orthodox church near Swedish airport for espionage
perspectives.summary: Swedish intelligence services suspect Moscow is using a Russian Orthodox church near Vasteras airport as a platform for espionage and hybrid activities threatening Swedens national security.

headlines.headline: US Justice Department Drops Federal Reserve Investigation
headlines.summary: The DOJ has ended its criminal investigation into Fed Chair Jerome Powell, clearing the path for Kevin Warsh's confirmation. A judge earlier quashed subpoenas citing "essentially zero evidence" of criminal conduct.

perspectives.angle: Trump Justice Department adds firing squads for federal executions
perspectives.summary: The US Department of Justice added electrocution, gas chamber, and firing squads as execution methods, after President Trump called for expanding the use of the death penalty.

perspectives.angle: US Justice Department drops criminal probe into Federal Reserve Chair Powell
perspectives.summary: The Justice Department has dropped its criminal investigation into Fed Chair Jerome Powell over renovation costs. The move likely clears the way for Kevin Warsh to potentially succeed Powell.

perspectives.angle: Justice Department drops criminal probe of Fed Chair Powell
perspectives.summary: President Trump had accused Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell of improper cost overruns in renovating the Fed building. The probe clearance likely opens the way for a potential replacement.

perspectives.angle: US Justice Department drops criminal probe into Federal Reserve Chair Powell
perspectives.summary: The Department of Justice has dropped its criminal investigation into Fed Chairman Jerome Powell, clearing the path for confirmation of his successor Kevin Warsh. Trump had accused Powell of improper cost overruns.

perspectives.angle: US Justice Department drops criminal probe of Fed Chair Jerome Powell
perspectives.summary: The DOJ ended its investigation into the Federal Reserve chairman after President Trump accused Powell of improper cost overruns in renovating the Fed building. The move clears the path for Powell successor Kevin Warsh confirmation.

perspectives.angle: US Justice Department drops probe into Federal Reserve Chair Powell
perspectives.summary: The DOJ dropped its investigation into Fed Chair Jerome Powell after Trump accused him of cost overruns in renovating the Fed building. The move clears the path for confirmation of his successor Kevin Warsh.

perspectives.angle: US Justice Department Drops Probe into Fed Chair Powell
perspectives.summary: The DOJ dropped its investigation into Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell after Trump accused him of cost overruns in Fed building renovations.

headlines.headline: UK Reaffirms Falklands Sovereignty After US Review Report
headlines.summary: Downing Street insisted the Falkland Islands belong to the UK after reports the Pentagon was considering options to punish NATO allies. An internal email suggested reviewing the US position on Britain's claim to the territory.

headlines.headline: NATO pushes back at reported US threat to suspend Spain over Iran war support
headlines.summary: An internal Pentagon email reportedly outlined options to punish allies perceived as lacking support for the Iran war, including suspending Spain from NATO. European allies strongly rejected the notion.

perspectives.angle: Spain Rejects NATO Suspension Threat, Says It Is Reliable Member
perspectives.summary: Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez defended Spain's NATO credentials after reports the Pentagon considered suspending allies refusing to support Iran operations. Italy and France also refused overflight rights for US military aircraft deployed for the Middle East conflict.

perspectives.angle: Europe pushes back at reported US threat to suspend Spain from NATO
perspectives.summary: NATO officials say there is no provision to expel members after an internal Pentagon email reportedly outlined options to punish allies over perceived lack of support for the Iran war.

headlines.headline: Bolsonaro son launches presidential bid to free father from prison
headlines.summary: Flavio Bolsonaro announces candidacy for Brazilian president with explicit goal of freeing his father Jair Bolsonaro, who is currently imprisoned. The move sets up a high-stakes election battle against Lula.

headlines.headline: US sanctions China-based oil refinery in sweeping crackdown on Iranian oil trade
headlines.summary: Treasury targets China teapot refinery and 40 shipping entities for purchasing Iranian oil, expanding enforcement against Tehrans shadow trade network amid the ongoing conflict.

perspectives.angle: US sanctions China-based oil refinery and 40 shippers over Iran oil trade
perspectives.summary: The US imposed sanctions on Hengli Petrochemical's Dalian facility, one of China's largest independent refineries with 400,000 barrels per day capacity, along with 40 shipping entities for processing and transporting Iranian crude.

perspectives.angle: US sanctions China-based Hengli refinery and 40 shippers over Iran oil
perspectives.summary: The US imposed sanctions on Hengli Petrochemical facility in Dalian, one of China largest independent refineries processing 400,000 barrels per day, along with 40 shipping entities for trading Iranian oil.

headlines.headline: Iran Executes Alleged Israeli Agent Over Sabotage During Protests
headlines.summary: Iran executed a man accused of acting as an Israeli agent and carrying out sabotage operations during nationwide protests. The execution highlights ongoing tensions between Iran and Israel.

headlines.headline: US Air Force Aims to Double F-15EX Eagle Fleet to 267 Aircraft
headlines.summary: The US Air Force plans to boost F-15EX Eagle II inventory to 267 aircraft, doubling the original program size. The move reflects growing demand for advanced fighter capabilities amid global tensions.

headlines.headline: UK Greens Leader Slams Starmer Over 'Weaponizing' Antisemitism
headlines.summary: UK Green Party co-leader Carla Denyer criticized Prime Minister Keir Starmer for what she called 'weaponizing' antisemitism after a parliamentary rebuke. The exchange highlights tensions over Middle East policy in British politics.

headlines.headline: Europe Prepares for Prolonged Ukraine War With No Clear End Strategy
headlines.summary: With US attention focused on Iran, neither Russia nor Ukraine has a path to victory or negotiation. European leaders are bracing for a protracted conflict as American diplomatic resources shift elsewhere.

headlines.headline: Everest flood warning system abandoned, thousands at risk
headlines.summary: A multi-million dollar flood warning system at Imja glacial lake near Everest has not been maintained since 2016, leaving local communities vulnerable to outburst floods.

headlines.headline: Chinese EV giant BYD says it can thrive without US market
headlines.summary: Chinas largest electric vehicle manufacturer positions itself to benefit from the global shift away from fossil fuels as fuel prices rise and geopolitical tensions shape trade.

headlines.headline: Pirates hijack oil tanker off Somalia coast
headlines.summary: Piracy in the Indian Ocean has made a comeback in recent years, with a vessel seized in a region once notorious for maritime attacks.

headlines.headline: Ukraine to Field 25,000 Ground Robots for Frontline Logistics
headlines.summary: Ukraines military plans to deploy 25,000 unmanned ground vehicles to replace soldiers in dangerous logistics missions. Ground robots ran more than 9,000 frontline missions in March, triple Novembers count.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine to field 25,000 ground robots for frontline logistics
perspectives.summary: Ukraine plans to deploy 25,000 unmanned ground vehicles to replace soldiers in dangerous logistics missions. Ground robots ran over 9,000 frontline missions in March alone, triple November count.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine to field 25,000 ground robots for frontline logistics
perspectives.summary: Ukraines military plans to deploy 25,000 ground robots to replace soldiers for frontline logistics operations. Ground robots ran more than 9,000 missions in March, triple Novembers count, and captured their first Russian position last summer.

headlines.headline: Seven Dead as Russian Strike Hits Residential Building in Dnipro
headlines.summary: A major Russian attack on Ukraine killed at least seven people, with the city of Dnipro hardest hit after a strike on a residential building. Four deaths were reported from the Dnipro attack alone.

perspectives.angle: Eight killed in Russian mass strike on Dnipro and multiple cities
perspectives.summary: Russia launched one of its largest aerial attacks on Ukraine, killing at least 8 and injuring 60 across Dnipro and other cities. The assault involved missiles and drones targeting multiple locations overnight.

perspectives.angle: 12 Russian FSB officers killed in drone strike on command post in occupied Donetsk
perspectives.summary: Ukrainian forces used FP-2 drones with 60-100kg payloads to strike an FSB command post, killing 12 officers in a significant intelligence blow to Russian security services.

headlines.headline: US Sanctions China-Based Oil Refinery and 40 Shippers Over Iranian Oil
headlines.summary: The US Treasury Department imposed sanctions on a China-based oil refinery and 40 shipping companies for handling Iranian oil, escalating economic pressure on Tehran amid the ongoing conflict.

perspectives.angle: US Sanctions Chinese Refinery for Buying Iranian Oil, Funding Military
perspectives.summary: The Treasury Department targeted Hengli refinery for generating hundreds of millions in revenue for Iran's military operations through illicit oil sales, stepping up enforcement of sanctions.

perspectives.angle: US Sanctions Major Chinese Refinery and 40 Shippers Over Iranian Oil
